description: Individual stimulus items within each assessment section (217 items across all instruments)

data.stimulus_content: When the sunlight strikes raindrops in the air, they act as a prism and form a rainbow. (Full Rainbow Passage — standardised text)
Tell me about a typical day in your life or something you enjoy doing.

I feel frustrated when I stutter.

Patient sustains /a/ for as long as possible on one breath. Record maximum phonation time.
Patient sustains /i/ for as long as possible on one breath. Record maximum phonation time.
Patient sustains /u/ for as long as possible on one breath. Record maximum phonation time.
Patient repeats /pa/ as fast as possible for 10 repetitions. Time total and assess regularity.
Patient repeats /ta/ as fast as possible for 10 repetitions. Time total and assess regularity.
Patient repeats /ka/ as fast as possible for 10 repetitions. Time total and assess regularity.
Patient repeats /pataka/ as fast as possible for 10 repetitions. Time total and assess regularity.

The therapist carefully assessed the patient's prosody.

Patient reads the Rainbow Passage into a calibrated microphone (44.1 kHz, 16-bit). Praat analysis extracts: syllable rate (syl/s), articulation rate (syl/s excl. pauses), mean pause duration (ms), and pause count.
Patient speaks for 3 minutes on a given topic. Same Praat acoustic analysis repeated for naturalistic speech comparison.
Patient names 50 pictures. Each response scored as optimal (correct, intelligible, no distortion) or non-optimal.
Patient describes 10 pictures in sentences. Each sentence scored as intelligible or not by a naive listener.
Which three words go together? apple / run / mango / orange
Which three words go together? car / bicycle / drive / bus
Which three words go together? hot / boil / cold / freeze
Which three words go together? doctor / hospital / patient / street
Which three words go together? happy / sad / angry / feeling

The committee, having carefully reviewed all the evidence, decided to postpone their final decision.
